Styles › Prairie Style

1900 – 1920 · USA Midwest

Prairie Style

Long low horizontals married to the open prairie.

  • Overview
  • Design Philosophy
  • Visual Characteristics
  • Materials
  • Color Palette
  • Façade Guide
  • Lighting
  • Building Types
  • Design Checklist
  • Common Mistakes
  • AI Prompts
  • Gallery
  • Related

01 — Overview

Overview

Definition. Prairie Style is an early American modern movement of strong horizontal lines, low-pitched roofs with broad eaves and bands of windows that echo the flat Midwestern landscape.

Historical background. Pioneered by Frank Lloyd Wright and the Prairie School, it broke from Victorian verticality toward open, ground-hugging houses rooted in their site.

Origin. American Midwest, c.1900–1920.

Timeline

1901

Wright's 'A Home in a Prairie Town'

1908

Robie House

1909

Prairie School at its height

1915

Influence spreads nationally

Key Architects

Frank Lloyd WrightGeorge MaherWilliam Gray PurcellMarion Mahony Griffin

Influential Projects

Robie House

Frank Lloyd Wright · 1910
Chicago, USA

Meyer May House

Meyer May House

Frank Lloyd Wright · 1909
Grand Rapids, USA

Westcott House

Westcott House

Frank Lloyd Wright · 1908
Springfield, USA

02 — Design Philosophy

Design Philosophy

Core Principles

HorizontalityConnection to the landOpen planIntegral ornamentBroad sheltering roofs

The Thinking

The house lies low against the prairie: emphatic horizontal bands, deep eaves and ribbon windows tie it to the ground and the open horizon.

Values It Represents

BelongingOpennessCraftDomesticity

Why It Emerged

A break from Victorian fussiness toward an authentically American, ground-rooted, horizontal home for the open Midwest.

03 — Visual Characteristics

Visual Characteristics

MassingLong, low, ground-hugging masses with strong horizontal banding.
Form LanguageHorizontal extruded forms with broad overhanging hipped roofs.
CompositionCantilevered eaves and terraces layered horizontally.
ProportionEmphatically horizontal and human-scaled.
GeometryOrthogonal with banded geometry.
RhythmContinuous horizontal bands of brick, sill and eave.
SymmetryOften symmetrical about a massive central chimney.
ScaleDomestic and grounded.
Roof TypesLow-pitched hipped roofs with very broad overhanging eaves.
Window ProportionRibbon windows in horizontal bands; art-glass casements.
EntranceSheltered, indirect, tucked beneath the eaves.
Façade ArticulationRoman brick, banded sills, broad eaves and ribbon art-glass windows.

04 — Materials

Materials

Roman brick

Robust, modular and warm — the everyday material of solid walls.

Limestone bands

Solid, durable and rooted in place — a defining exterior material of the style.

Stucco

Smooth render that unifies the wall and catches light.

Timber trim

Warm, renewable and tactile — softens and clads the exterior.

Art glass

Admits light and dissolves the wall between inside and out.

05 — Color Palette

Color Palette

Earthy and warm — autumnal browns, golds and greens of the prairie.

Primary

Brick Tan
#b08a64
Warm Ochre
#caa84a

Accent

Forest Green
#46584b
Autumn Gold
#b58a2a

Material Colors

Limestone
#d8cdb8
Timber
#7a5a3a

Typical Combinations

  • +Roman brick + limestone + green
  • +Ochre + brown + art-glass amber

06 — Façade Design Guide

Façade Design Guide

Window CompositionRibbon windows in continuous horizontal bands; geometric art glass.
EmphasisStrongly horizontal — banded brick and broad eaves.
Shading DevicesVery broad overhanging eaves shade the windows.
BalconiesCantilevered terraces and low planters.
FramesTimber-trimmed art-glass casements.
OpeningsBands of windows wrapping corners.
Architectural DetailsBanded brick, limestone copings, geometric art glass.
OrnamentationIntegral and geometric — abstracted natural motifs in art glass and trim.

07 — Lighting

Lighting

Natural Lighting Strategy

Deep eaves soften light into banded ribbon windows; warm and sheltered.

Artificial Lighting Concept

Warm, low, integral lighting; art-glass lanterns.

Night Façade Appearance

Warm glow from ribbon windows beneath dark eaves.

Recommendations

Warm 2700K; light the eave underside; glow the art glass.

08 — Typical Building Types

Typical Building Types

Where this style performs best:

HousesClubhousesLibrariesBanksSchoolsCivic buildings

09 — Design Checklist

Design Checklist

Tap an item to mark it complete — saved to your browser.

  • Make the massing long and low
  • Use broad overhanging hipped roofs
  • Band windows horizontally
  • Anchor with a massive central chimney
  • Use Roman brick and limestone bands
  • Add geometric art glass
  • Cantilever terraces and planters
  • Keep an earthy autumnal palette

10 — Common Mistakes

Common Mistakes

Elements that contradict the style — what to avoid:

  • ✕Vertical, tall proportions
  • ✕Steep complex roofs
  • ✕Punched single windows instead of bands
  • ✕Applied historical ornament
  • ✕Cold palettes denying the earthy warmth

11 — AI Prompt Examples

AI Prompt Examples

Optimised for Midjourney, ChatGPT image tools or Claude — exterior renders. Tap copy to use.

Exterior

Façade Concept

Prairie style façade, horizontal brick banding, ribbon windows, broad eaves, limestone copings, geometric art glass, frontal elevation, warm light

Night View

Prairie style house exterior at night, warm glow from ribbon windows beneath dark eaves, lit art glass, architectural night photography

Aerial View

Aerial of a prairie style house, long low hipped roofs, terraces, gardens, flat landscape, drone photography

Street Perspective

Street view of a prairie style house exterior, horizontal banding, broad eaves, art-glass windows, mature trees, documentary photography

Masterplan

Prairie style estate masterplan, low horizontal houses, terraces and gardens on flat land, axonometric, earthy palette
